review_text: Held up through a week on the Pacific Crest. Zippers still smooth, no frayed seams, and the hip-belt pocket fits my phone. The only nit is the water-bottle pocket is tight with a Nalgene.
Kept me dry in three days of Oregon rain. The pit zips are a lifesaver. Hood adjustment is fiddly with gloves on.
Bright enough for camp. The battery door squeaks and popped open once in a pack.
Pitched it solo in 4 minutes on my second try. Two people plus gear fit without any wrestling.
Real warm for 11 oz. Cuffs curl up after a few washes, which looks rough.
Leaked by the third wet hike. Returning — support was fast.
Flick locks haven't slipped once, even with a 35-lb pack. Cork grips beat foam in humid weather.
Boils half a liter in under three minutes. Flame gets finicky above 9k ft.
Packed it underwater by accident on a river day. Not a drop inside.
Comfortable at 35F. Tried it at 25F and I was cold by 4 AM, despite the rating.
Great mid-layer. Pills around the collar after a month.
Best trail runner I've worn. Grippy on wet rock, drains water fast.
Took a beating on a rental truck and came out clean. Bomb-proof zippers.
Zipper stuck on day one. Low-rated for a flagship wind jacket.
Kept coffee hot for 3 hours on a cold morning. Lid drips if you don't seat it just right.
Polarized lenses are sharp, and the nose pads don't slide on sweaty days.
Kept snow out on a knee-deep approach. Strap buckle is plastic and I worry about it.
Perfect for a day hike. The hidden back pocket is great for keys and a snack.
Warm, but wet through after an hour in wet snow.
No blisters on a 20-mile day. Washed hot by mistake and they still fit.
